title: The Desert of Religion
summary: CME00075 is the repository XML for a University of Michigan manually keyed and proofread electronic text derived from two separately paginated installments in Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 126 (1911): Walter Hübner's manuscript description, edited poem, and afterword on pp. 58-74, and K. Schreiner's textual and linguistic criticism followed by Hübner's source study on pp. 360-364. Hübner based his continuous 940-line text on British Library Add MS 37049, ff. 46r-66v, and collated Cotton MS Faustina B VI, part II, ff. 3r-23v, and Stowe MS 39, now cataloged at ff. 10r-31v. The XML retains Hübner's textual apparatus and the prose studies but omits the source volume's two audited manuscript-image plates and their blank backs. A complete public scan of Archiv 126 is cached and maps both installments and the two plate leaves. A complete public scan, OCR, metadata, and scan-data set for Archiv 127 is cached around Hope Emily Allen's pp. 388-390 addendum, which identifies extensive direct borrowing from the Speculum vitae and other sources; this later study is collateral evidence, not an ancestor of the XML. Anne Mouron's complete public 2016 article is also cached as modern scholarly context. The British Library reports full legacy digital coverage for Add MS 37049, but the exact viewer and inferred new IIIF endpoint were unavailable when checked; Stowe's legacy images are likewise unavailable, and no complete public Cotton surrogate was found. Exact catalog, onsite consultation, complete-object reproduction-order, and permissions routes are recorded for all three manuscripts.
review_notes: Hübner's title is an editorial title inferred from line 19; the medieval witnesses are not treated as having a verified title rubric.
Both immediate installments belong to Archiv volume 126. Archiv volume 127 is modeled only as the carrier of Hope Emily Allen's later addendum.
The three manuscripts are distinct illustrated witnesses. Richard Rolle's portrait and quoted verses are iconographic and source-context evidence, not evidence that Rolle authored the whole poem.

The requested reproduction scope is the complete present physical object or separately bound component, with the work's folios identified inside it; no cropped work-only manuscript cache was substituted.

entities.label: General Corpus source XML for CME00075
entities.description: The XML artifact consumed by this repository's conversion and publication workflow, supplied through the Kyvero-Press/CME git submodule.
notes: IDG/@ID, BIBNO, and VID are all CME00075.
The 2016-09-08 TEMPHEAD record documents structural review and proof corrections but not the item's original keying date.

entities.label: University of Michigan CME item CME00075
entities.description: A public manually keyed and proofread electronic text that serializes the 1911 poem, apparatus, manuscript description, afterword, textual criticism, linguistic discussion, and source study while omitting the printed manuscript-image plates.

entities.label: Walter Hübner, The Desert of Religion, part I (1911)
entities.description: The first installment of Hübner's edition: manuscript descriptions and attribution history, a 940-line base text drawn from Add MS 37049 with Cotton and Stowe variants in footnotes, and an afterword on the illustrated Richard Rolle tradition. The audited source scan also contains two unpaginated monochrome manuscript plates and two blank plate backs that are not represented in the XML.

bibliographic.citation: Walter Hübner, 'The desert of religion. Mit dem Bilde des Richard Rolle of Hampole. Nach drei Handschriften zum erstenmal herausgegeben. I. Handschriftenbeschreibung und Text,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 126 (1911), 58-74, with two audited unpaginated manuscript-image plates.
bibliographic.title: The desert of religion. Mit dem Bilde des Richard Rolle of Hampole. Nach drei Handschriften zum erstenmal herausgegeben. I. Handschriftenbeschreibung und Text
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

physical_description.extent: printed pp. 58-74, two unpaginated manuscript-image plates, and their blank backs within Archiv volume 126
notes: In the cached PDF the article occupies PDF pp. 71-91; the Cotton plate is PDF p. 72 with its blank back at p. 73, and the Stowe plate is PDF p. 79 with its blank back at p. 78.
The article begins on printed p. 58 and is signed 'Walter Hübner' at the end of printed p. 74.

entities.label: K. Schreiner and Walter Hübner, The Desert of Religion, part II (1911)
entities.description: The separately paginated continuation in the same Archiv volume. Schreiner's signed section analyzes manuscript relationships, rhyme, and Northern language and argues that A has the fewest errors and should underlie the text; Hübner's signed section analyzes biblical, patristic, and other sources.

bibliographic.citation: K. Schreiner and Walter Hübner, 'The desert of religion. II. Handschriftenkritik. Reim und Sprache. Zur Quellenkunde,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 126 (1911), 360-364.
bibliographic.title: The desert of religion. II. Handschriftenkritik. Reim und Sprache. Zur Quellenkunde
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

physical_description.format: Journal article continuation and scholarly commentary
notes: In the cached PDF the continuation occupies PDF pp. 380-384.
Schreiner signs after the language discussion on printed p. 362; Hübner signs after the source study on p. 364.

entities.label: London, British Library, Add MS 37049
entities.description: The illustrated Northern English Carthusian miscellany used as Hübner's base witness. The Desert of Religion occupies ff. 46r-66v and is accompanied by 22 miniatures and 20 full-page tree diagrams; Richard Rolle is identified at f. 52v.

holding.notes: The exact current British Library record describes the whole volume and its target folios.

physical_description.extent: ff. i + 96, plus four unfoliated paper flyleaves at the beginning and four at the end
physical_description.dimensions: 270 x 200 mm; text space approximately 230-235 x 140-160 mm
notes: The current catalog gives a Yorkshire or Lincolnshire Carthusian monastery as a possible origin and describes the dialect as Northern.
Scott's costume dating places the manuscript after about 1460-1470; the catalog normalizes the range to 1460-1500.
The target work is ff. 46r-66v. Hübner's historical broad locator 'fol. 46-67' includes the immediately following f. 67 material, which the current catalog treats separately.

entities.label: London, British Library, Cotton MS Faustina B VI, part II
alternate_labels: Cotton MS Faustina B VI, part II, ff. 1-23
entities.description: A separately bound parchment component containing Vado mori at ff. 1v-2r and The Desert of Religion at ff. 3r-23v, including the Twelve Abuses at f. 8r and Richard Rolle verses at f. 8v. Hübner collated it as C and reproduced its Richard Rolle page.

identifiers.value: Cotton MS Faustina B VI, part II

holding.shelfmark: Cotton MS Faustina B VI, part II, ff. 1-23
holding.notes: The component-level record supplies the exact work boundary and should be used in requests; the parent record describes the composite Cotton volume and the separate 1892 binding of part II.

physical_description.extent: 23 folios in the separately bound part II component
notes: The component record assigns origin to North England.
The Desert of Religion occupies ff. 3r-23v; the complete reproduction request should cover all ff. 1-23 and all binding/endleaf surfaces of the present part II object, not only the target work.
Hübner reports a historical Walter Hilton attribution and reproduces part II f. 8v, historically cited as fol. 8b.

entities.label: London, British Library, Stowe MS 39
entities.description: An illustrated manuscript containing The Abbey of the Holy Ghost and The Desert of Religion. The current catalog locates the target at ff. 10r-31v and records that the quires are no longer in their original order.

holding.notes: The current record supplies whole-object codicology, current target folios, legacy digital-content status, and onsite conditions.

physical_description.support: parchment, with paper only at ff. 1-2 and 34
physical_description.extent: 34 folios, one unfoliated paper flyleaf at the beginning, and two at the end
physical_description.dimensions: 265 x 190 mm; written space 205 x 150 mm
notes: The target occupies current ff. 10r-31v; Hübner used the historical locator fols. 11-32.

The catalog says the original order was The Desert of Religion at ff. 33, 10-32 followed by The Abbey of the Holy Ghost at ff. 4-9; this codicological statement does not enlarge the current cataloged target-text boundary.
Thomas Astle's 1768 notes incorrectly attribute the manuscript's Middle English texts to John Alcock.
The current catalog records possible production for a Benedictine nunnery but gives origin only as England.

entities.label: BCU Iaşi complete provider scan of Archiv volume 126 (1911)
entities.description: A 531-page PDF of the complete provider-published volume, including Hübner's two installments and the two unpaginated manuscript-image plates. The scan has page-level library watermarks and embedded OCR.

physical_description.format: Complete provider-published bound-volume PDF with OCR
notes: Completeness is relative to the provider's published volume scan; no claim is made that preservation masters or every binding exterior were exposed.

The DSpace metadata misidentifies the editors as Aloys Bronde and Adolf Tobler and the language as Romanian; the scanned title page identifies Alois Brandl and Heinrich Morf and the volume itself is principally German.

entities.label: Hope Emily Allen, The Desert of Religion: Addendum (1911)
entities.description: A later source study responding to Hübner's edition. Allen maps extensive line ranges to the Speculum vitae, identifies additional borrowings from The Prick of Conscience, and proposes sources for the Twelve Abuses and other passages. It is not upstream of Hübner or the CME encoding.

bibliographic.citation: Hope Emily Allen, 'The desert of religion: Addendum,' Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen 127 (1911), 388-390.
bibliographic.title: The desert of religion: Addendum
bibliographic.container_title: Archiv für das Studium der neueren Sprachen und Literaturen

entities.label: Internet Archive complete provider scan of Archiv volume 127 (1911)
entities.description: The complete 512-page PDF exposed for Internet Archive item archivfrdasstu127brauuoft, with complete-volume OCR, metadata, and scan-data sidecars cached around Allen's addendum.

physical_description.extent: 512 PDF pages; Internet Archive metadata records 516 scanned images
notes: The difference between 512 PDF pages and 516 source images reflects derivative packaging; the cached scan-data file preserves the provider's inventory.
The item metadata's generic date field says 1846, but the scanned title page and copyright-evidence note both identify the volume as 1911.

entities.reproduction_format: Grayscale PDF page images with OCR and XML sidecars

entities.label: Anne Mouron, Praying with Images (2016)
entities.description: A public modern scholarly article that describes the work as a 943-line fifteenth-century devotional poem surviving in three Northern English illustrated manuscripts, explains its 22 conceptual sections and twenty tree diagrams, identifies major source texts, and analyzes its monastic and possibly Carthusian reading context.

bibliographic.citation: Anne Mouron, 'Praying with Images: Some Medieval Advice,' The Way 55, no. 1 (January 2016), 20-30.
bibliographic.title: Praying with Images: Some Medieval Advice

agents.name: K. Schreiner
notes: The source supplies only an initial and surname; no expanded identity was adopted without authority evidence.

access.access_method: Download the complete article PDF from The Way.
Open the exact British Library whole-volume record.
The current catalog links a legacy complete digital object, but the host was unavailable; the inferred restored IIIF manifest endpoint returned HTTP 403.
access.format: Legacy complete page-image object; current deliverable unavailable
notes: The catalog explicitly says full digital coverage exists, but no complete image or manifest deliverable could be retrieved and therefore nothing was cached.
Do not substitute the two printed Hübner plates or isolated third-party images for this complete manuscript object.

access.access_method: Register for a Reader Pass, request the exact shelfmark, and consult in the London Reading Room; restrictions and a letter of introduction apply.
Use the British Library Digitisation Services form and request a complete color photographic surrogate of the present object, not only ff. 46r-66v.
access.contact: licensing@bl.uk for digitisation-service reuse questions; permissions@bl.uk for Imaging Services permission after or alongside the order
access.format: Digital reference or reproduction photography; availability, handling, format, color, and price subject to quote
notes: Request covers, spine, boards, pastedowns, every flyleaf and blank, and ff. i + 1-96 recto and verso; identify ff. 46r-66v as the work portion.
The service says original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned and that existing surrogates may otherwise yield black-and-white images; specify color explicitly.
Delivery does not authorize repository redistribution or print publication. Obtain written permission for the proposed Git and print-on-demand uses.

access.access_method: Open the exact part II component record; use the parent record for composite-volume physical context.
The exact current component record has no Digitised Content link; only selected reproductions such as Hübner's f. 8v plate were verified.
notes: This is a dated public-search result, not a claim that the manuscript has never been photographed.

notes: The provider statement is jurisdiction-specific and is not a worldwide dedication.

notes: This does not override the repository's licensing of new project-authored metadata, code, formatting, or outputs.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Three illustrated witnesses with independent errors; C and S share errors attributed to a common exemplar.
editorial_practices.representation: A continuous base text from Add MS 37049 with C and S variants, omissions, and alternative readings in 624 XML-encoded footnotes.
editorial_practices.description: Hübner's printed edition and Schreiner's continuation use Add MS 37049 as the base because it has the fewest errors, while collating Cotton and Stowe throughout. The stemma does not claim that any surviving witness directly copies another.
editorial_practices.scope: Hübner's 940-line main text and its textual apparatus

editorial_practices.source_feature: Two article runs in one journal volume, with two unpaginated manuscript-image plates and their blank backs embedded in the first run.
editorial_practices.representation: The XML encodes printed pp. 58-74 and 360-364, retains plate-number gaps in PB/REF values, and represents only the shield and stemma as FIGURE structures; it does not include the plate images.
editorial_practices.description: The digital item is a selective text encoding of the complete 1911 source articles, not a page-image facsimile of the journal or any manuscript.

evidence.citation: CME/source/CME_phase_3/CME00075.xml at CME submodule commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c
evidence.summary: Provides the exact repository artifact, internal identifiers, language declaration, title, encoded page references, text, apparatus, contributors, revision record, and repository-layer checksum.

evidence.citation: Computational and structural inspection of CME00075.xml
evidence.summary: Counts 2 DIV1, 5 DIV2, 22 PB, 2 FIGURE, 19 LG, 954 L, 625 NOTE, and 40 P elements. The main poem DIV2 has 940 L elements; the afterword has 14 quoted verse lines. PB refs expose two two-surface gaps corresponding to omitted plate leaves.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, CME00075 stable item
evidence.summary: Supplies the stable public collection identifier and access route for the electronic text.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library, Corpus of Middle English Prose and Verse collection and About pages
evidence.summary: States that collection batches were manually keyed from public-domain editions, proofread in house, distributed in XML, and marked public domain in the United States under No Copyright-United States.

evidence.citation: Walter Hübner, 'The desert of religion,' and K. Schreiner and Walter Hübner, continuation, Archiv 126 (1911), 58-74 and 360-364
evidence.locator: PDF pp. 71-91 and 380-384; printed pp. 58-74 and 360-364
evidence.summary: Direct inspection establishes the exact two-installment bibliography, signatures, three-witness key, Add base text, C and S collation, stemma, Northern language argument, source study, 940-line lineation, and article boundaries.

evidence.citation: Page-by-page visual audit of the cached Archiv 126 PDF around Hübner's first installment
evidence.summary: Confirms a Cotton part II f. 8v plate on PDF p. 72 with blank back p. 73 and a Stowe historical fol. 30a plate on PDF p. 79 with blank back p. 78. These four surfaces account for the XML PB/REF gaps; neither manuscript image is encoded.

evidence.citation: Biblioteca Centrală Universitară Mihai Eminescu Iaşi DSpace record for Archiv 126 (1911)
evidence.summary: Provides the public item landing page, complete PDF file, handle, provider ownership statement, and bibliographic metadata. Direct title-page inspection corrects the record's editor and language errors.

evidence.citation: Hope Emily Allen, 'The desert of religion: Addendum,' Archiv 127 (1911), 388-390
evidence.summary: Direct inspection confirms Allen's signed three-page addendum, its response to Hübner, and its line-by-line identification of Speculum vitae and other source borrowings.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive item archivfrdasstu127brauuoft and exact metadata sidecar
evidence.summary: Supplies item identity, title, volume 127, scan provenance, 516-image count, ARK, public derivatives, and the provider's NOT_IN_COPYRIGHT/US classification. The internal title page, not the erroneous generic metadata date 1846, proves the 1911 issue date.

evidence.citation: Anne Mouron, 'Praying with Images: Some Medieval Advice,' The Way 55/1 (2016), 20-30
evidence.summary: Provides a modern specialist account of the title's editorial origin, three Northern English illustrated witnesses, 943-line modern lineation, 22 sections, twenty trees, major sources, probable monastic audience, and possible but unproved Carthusian authorship/context. Mouron's footnote cites Hübner part I as pp. 56-74, but direct inspection shows the installment begins on p. 58; pp. 56-57 complete the preceding Orm article.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Add MS 37049
evidence.summary: Supplies the whole manuscript's current shelfmark, physical extent, support, date, possible Carthusian origin, exact target ff. 46r-66v, illustration inventory, Richard Rolle locus, current access conditions, LARK, and claim of full legacy digital coverage currently unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Faustina B VI, part II, ff. 1-23
evidence.summary: Supplies the exact component identity, target ff. 3r-23v, incorporated Twelve Abuses and Rolle verses, 1425-1449 date, North England origin, current access conditions, and absence of a Digitised Content link.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Cotton MS Faustina B VI parent record
evidence.summary: Supplies the composite hierarchy and confirms that part II is parchment, approximately 265 x 210 mm, and separately bound in a British Museum 1892 binding.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Stowe MS 39
evidence.summary: Supplies current target ff. 10r-31v, first-half-fifteenth-century date, whole-volume extent and support, illustration inventory, altered quire order, Astle attribution history, letter-of-introduction condition, and legacy digital images currently unavailable.

evidence.citation: British Library, Digitised manuscripts and archives and cyber-attack recovery update
evidence.summary: States that some digitised manuscripts remain unavailable because of the cyber-attack and that digital collection access remains limited during recovery.

Provides the current online order route, required shelfmark/folio information, reference-versus-reproduction image distinction, manuscript photography rule, color-request instruction, fee basis, and warning to seek permission for reuse.

evidence.citation: British Library, Imaging Services terms and conditions, updated January 2026
evidence.summary: States processing and pricing conditions, places third-party rights clearance on the customer, and says supplied material must not be copied or reproduced without written permission, which may carry additional fees.

open_questions.question: When will complete public image deliverables for Add MS 37049 and Stowe MS 39 return, and does a complete digital surrogate exist for the separately bound Cotton part II component?
next_steps: Recheck the exact Add LARK/IIIF endpoint and Stowe legacy object after British Library recovery updates.
Ask British Library Reference Services whether a complete current Cotton part II surrogate exists but is not linked in the interim catalog.
If no public restoration date is available, order complete color photographic surrogates using the object-wide scopes recorded here.
notes: No manuscript image file was cached because no complete public deliverable was retrievable.

open_questions.question: What exact written permissions and fees would govern Git redistribution, public download, scholarly quotation, and commercial or nonprofit print-on-demand use of any newly supplied British Library photographs?
next_steps: Describe the repository's public and commercial enablement purpose in the order or follow-up message.

Ask licensing@bl.uk and permissions@bl.uk to distinguish private research use, public Git hosting, illustration in scholarship, derivative formatting research, and image reproduction in print-on-demand books.
Preserve the written response as item-specific rights evidence before publishing any delivered files.

open_questions.question: Why does Hübner's encoded main text contain 940 line elements while Mouron's modern edition lineation describes a 943-line poem?
next_steps: Consult Mouron's completed DPhil edition or forthcoming EETS edition and compare its lineation against Hübner and all three witnesses.
Identify whether the three-line difference reflects supplied witness readings, framing verse, or a different treatment of visual captions.

open_questions.question: Can composition be localized and dated more precisely, and can the probable monastic or Carthusian author-compiler context be demonstrated rather than inferred?
next_steps: Consult Mouron's DPhil/EETS edition and the full linguistic and codicological studies of all three witnesses.
Keep Northern dialect, individual manuscript production, possible Carthusian context, and work composition as separate claims.
Do not revive the Walter Hilton, John Alcock, or Richard Rolle attributions without new evidence.

open_questions.question: What is the exact current folio corresponding to Hübner's Stowe plate caption 'fol. 16b, neben v. 283 ff.'?
next_steps: Verify the plate against restored or ordered Stowe images and the current pencil foliation.
Request a repository-confirmed old-to-current foliation concordance rather than extrapolating the one-folio offset across the altered quire order.
